title: fsm_expand deleting design if state register is not initialised
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.51+85 (git sha1 1113c8c95, g++ 11.4.0-1ubuntu1~22.04 -fPIC -O3)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

[example.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19693381/example.zip)

I'm sorry for including a large file because it is related to `fsm_expand`, I don't know how to create a minimal example. 

Running the following steps:
```
read_verilog -sv example.sv
hierarchy -auto-top; flatten; proc; opt -nodffe -nosdff; clean -purge
fsm -expand
opt -full
stat
```

And keep `current_state` on `line 1875` of the file as is will optimise away the whole design.

However, if we initialise `line 1875` with `logic [3:0] current_state = 4'b0;`, the module will remain. 

I can tell is depends on the `fsm_expand` as if running without the `-expnad` flag, the module will not get optimised. 

### Expected Behavior

The `fsm -expand` should not depend on the register initialisation nor on optimising the whole circuit away. 

### Actual Behavior

The design gets optimised away.
code_blocks:
read_verilog -sv example.sv
hierarchy -auto-top; flatten; proc; opt -nodffe -nosdff; clean -purge
fsm -expand
opt -full
stat
